senses.definition: used to describe a person who is considered lucky

examples.x: He's such a lucky Devil that he'll probably win the lottery someday.
She landed a job at her dream company without even trying; she's a real lucky Devil.
With his charm and good looks, he had no trouble getting free upgrades at the hotel; he was considered a lucky Devil by his friends.

senses.definition: used to emphasize how lucky a person is or how successful they are
used to say that it is typical of a person to experience an unfortunate event

examples.x: Getting caught in the rain without an umbrella, story of her life, she thought.
He found himself stuck in another traffic jam during his morning commute, and he sighed, "Story of my life."
"I missed the bus again, story of my life," he muttered, frustrated with his chronic tardiness.
Losing his keys and frantically searching for them at the last minute was the story of his life.
She realized she had forgotten to buy the one item she needed from the grocery store, and she thought, "Typical, it's the story of my life."

senses.definition: used to say that one is used to something bad happening to one, due to having bad luck

examples.x: They sold the last ticket five minutes before I got there - just my luck!
After missing the last bus home, he muttered, "Just my luck," and prepared for a long walk.
She spilled coffee on her new white shirt just before an important meeting and exclaimed, "Well, just my luck!"
He had a flat tire on the way to a job interview, and he sighed, "Of course, just my luck."
She arrived at the ice cream shop to find her favorite flavor sold out, and she said, "Typical, just my luck."

senses.definition: said after a positive statement to hypothetically assure the continuation of good luck

examples.x: "I've never had a car accident, knock on wood," she said while tapping the table.
"I hope the good weather holds for our vacation, knock on wood," he said, tapping his head.
"I've never missed a flight, knock on wood," he exclaimed, rapping his knuckles on the door frame.
"Our team is on a winning streak, and I hope it continues, knock on wood," she said, tapping her desk.
"I've never been seriously ill, knock on wood," he said, giving the wooden table a friendly knock.

senses.definition: something that solely depends on chance

examples.x: In this raffle, winning is entirely the luck of the draw, so you never know.
Getting the best office cubicle wasn't a matter of skill; it was the luck of the draw.
We can't choose our roommates; it's all the luck of the draw in the dormitory.
The order in which the students present their projects will be the luck of the draw.
When it comes to jury duty, who gets selected is purely the luck of the draw.

senses.definition: to try to do or achieve something knowing that one might not succeed

examples.x: We just took pot luck.
We didn't plan our road trip's stops; we decided to take pot luck along the way.
I didn't have time to prepare lunch today, so I'll just take pot luck from the cafeteria.
When you're a guest in someone's home, it's polite to take pot luck with the meal they offer.

word_list.pronunciation: flˈaɪ baɪ ðə sˈiːt ʌv wˈʌnz pˈænts

senses.definition: to do something by solely relying on one's instincts, due to a lack of proper knowledge or experience

examples.x: I was flying by the seat of my pants because I'd never managed anybody before.
The truth is that neither experts nor mothers know as much as each might wish; to a great extent, all of us fly by the seat of our pants and try to learn quickly from experience.
The entrepreneur had no business plan but managed to succeed by flying by the seat of his pants, making decisions on the go.
I've had a tough week, but I finally caught a break when my lost wallet was returned.
After months of searching for a job, I finally caught a break and got an interview.
The team was losing the game, but they caught a break with a last-minute goal.
He was always struggling financially, but he finally caught a break when he received a unexpected bonus.
Amid all the setbacks, I just hope to catch a break and find some peace.

senses.definition: to have great success with something, particularly something that brings one a lot of money

examples.x: After years of searching for the perfect job, he finally struck gold with an amazing offer.
She invested in that startup, and it turned out to be a wise decision; she struck gold.
In the attic, he found an old painting that turned out to be a masterpiece, so he really struck gold.
The author's first novel was a hit, and she struck gold with her subsequent books.
Winning the lottery felt like striking gold; they couldn't believe their luck.
